methodology: Cross-reference network analysis (41-vote connection 1Pe1:19ŌåÆRev13:8, 18-vote Is53:7ŌåÆAct8:32) + patristic commentary reading (14 entries on Jn 1:29: Cyril, Chrysostom, Augustine; Bede chronological typology; Hippolytus on paschal lamb) + complete 8-link typological chain mapping (Gn 22 ŌåÆ Ex 12 ŌåÆ Is 53 ŌåÆ Jn 1 ŌåÆ 1Co 5 ŌåÆ 1Pe 1 ŌåÆ Rev 5 ŌåÆ Rev 13) + topic verification (LAMB_OF_GOD, LAMB, PASSOVER, BLOOD, ATONEMENT) + vocabulary variation analysis

evidence: commentaries (Cyril cites Is 53:7 directly, Chrysostom 'shadows of Law + prophecy of Isaiah', Augustine, Theophilus, Haydock ŌĆö 14 entries)
commentaries (Bede 'Lamb dying = Lion conquering', Haydock 'marks of passion in heaven ŌåÆ cites Jn 1:29', Irenaeus, Cyprian, Victorinus ŌĆö 8 entries)
commentaries (Bede chronological typology, Haydock 'paschal lamb prefigured Christ', Augustine, Jerome ŌĆö 4+2 entries)
commentaries (Clement, Lactantius, Tertullian, Apostolic Constitutions ŌĆö 4 entries)

new_refs_added.justification: Most direct identification: 'Cristo, nosso Cordeiro pascal, foi imolado.' 4 votes de EXO.12.5. 3 votes de ISA.53.7 and REV.5.6. Hippolytus, Tertullian, Chrysostom comment. Bridge verse: PassoverŌåÆChrist. Uses 'Cordeiro pascal' explicitly.

18 votes de ISA.53.7 (direct quotation). 10 votes de JHN.1.29. Haydock, Irenaeus, Chrysostom comment. Narrative bridge: Philip explains Is 53 to the Ethiopian eunuch as referring to Christ. McGarvey: Isaiah 53 is 'unintelligible without the gospel.'

Oldest prototype: 'God will provide for himself the lamb.' 7 votes para JHN.1.36. 6 votes para JHN.1.29. 5 votes para REV.5.12. 3 votes para REV.13.8. Ambrose and Ephrem comment. Abraham's answer is fulfilled ~2000 years later in John 1:29.

candidates_noted.justification: 41 votes de 1PE.1.19. 5 votes de REV.5.6. 'The Lamb slain from the foundation of the world.' Eternal dimension of the sacrifice ŌĆö the Lamb was 'slain' before creation.
6 votes para REV.4.11. 5 votes de GEN.22.8. Worship hymn: 'Worthy is the Lamb that was slain to receive power and riches and wisdom.' Liturgical culmination of the Lamb theme.

complete_typological_chain.description: 8-link typological chain ŌĆö the longest documented in this research

chain.content: 'God will provide the lamb' ŌĆö promise
Lamb without blemish, slain at Passover
'Like a lamb led to slaughter' ŌĆö suffering servant
'Behold the Lamb of God' ŌĆö John the Baptist
'Christ our Paschal Lamb has been sacrificed'
'Precious blood, like a lamb without blemish'
'Lamb slain from the foundation of the world'

complete_typological_chain.significance_for_tcc: A hybrid search for 'cordeiro de Deus' should ideally retrieve verses from ALL 8 links. BM25 finds 'cordeiro' in links 1,2,4,5,6,7,8 but misses link 3 (Isaiah uses 'ovelha'/sheep). Semantic search must connect the sacrificial concept across vocabulary variants.

augustine_triple_connection.description: Augustine connects three texts in a single sentence: Exodus 12 (bone not broken) + Isaiah 53:7 (lamb to slaughter) + John 19:31 (crucifixion). This demonstrates that the patristic tradition saw the Lamb typology as a unified chain, not isolated references.

vocabulary_variation.description: The Lamb concept uses different vocabulary across books, challenging lexical search
